# QuantPilot — Recipe Review Report

**Status:** TEMPLATE — run `/review-quant-recipe` to generate the actual report  
**Target file:** `docs/claude/recipe_review_report.md`  
**Generated by:** `backtest-forensics-agent`, `risk-gatekeeper-agent`, `source-curator-agent`

---

## How to Generate This Report

In a Claude Code session from the project root:

```
/review-quant-recipe <recipe-id>
```

If no recipe exists yet, run `/fable5-level34` first.

---

## Expected Report Structure

When generated, this file will contain:

```yaml
recipe_review:
  recipe_id: <string>
  review_date: <date>
  overall_verdict: approved | revise | rejected

  backtest_forensics:
    reviewer: backtest-forensics-agent
    overall_confidence: high | medium | low | reject
    deflated_sharpe: <float>
    pbo_score: <float>  # Probability of Backtest Overfitting; must be < 0.5
    findings:
      - category: data_integrity | statistical | microstructure | regime
        finding: <string>
        severity: critical | major | minor | info
        remediation: <string>

  risk_review:
    reviewer: risk-gatekeeper-agent
    decision: approved | revise | rejected
    findings:
      - parameter: <string>
        issue: <string>
        severity: critical | major | minor
        required_change: <string>

  source_review:
    reviewer: source-curator-agent
    findings:
      - signal: <string>
        citation_status: cited | uncited | weak
        recommendation: <string>

  required_changes: []  # Must be empty for overall_verdict: approved

  next_steps:
    - action: <string>
      owner: claude | codex | user
```

---

## Review Criteria Summary

| Dimension | Minimum Threshold |
|---|---|
| Out-of-sample Sharpe | ≥ 0.5 |
| Walk-forward windows | ≥ 5 |
| OOS period | ≥ 30% of total backtest period |
| Deflated Sharpe | ≥ 0.3 |
| PBO score | < 0.5 |
| Critical findings | 0 (any critical = reject) |
| Uncited signals | 0 (all signals must be cited) |
| Risk matrix circuit breakers | ≥ 2 |

---

## Disclaimer

A review verdict of "approved" means the recipe design meets minimum quality standards.  
It does not guarantee live trading profitability.  
Codex must implement and pass all acceptance tests before any execution begins.
